title: parartocarpus woodii
intro: Parartocarpus woodii, also known as the African star anise, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique star-shaped fruit and aromatic seeds. The seeds of the Parartocarpus woodii have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The seeds are commonly prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, and have also been used in traditional remedies for skin and wound care.
